findings.locus: §2.6 Governance boundary vs §5 / §6
findings.claim: Deliberately attacked whether the record smuggles a governance-policy or remedy-boundary ruling into the principle layer — the failure mode that would put this ADR at the wrong altitude and pre-empt the forthcoming feedback-loop governance design. §2.6 fences the remedy boundary (supersede vs retract), criteria, thresholds, cadence, retention windows, role assignments, and multi-condition composition to that downstream design and states 'The accountability principle this ADR commits holds identically whichever remedy applies, which is why the choice between them is policy, not principle, and is not ruled here.' §2 states 'It fixes no policy, threshold, cadence, or mechanism,' §6 check 5 (Boundary check) enforces the same non-smuggling rule, and §5.2 routes new commitments outward. No policy ruling is decided at the principle layer.
